key_strengths: Proprietary MOCVD process for InGaAs-on-12-inch-silicon with 10+ years of UCSB R&D lineage and ~35 patents
Government validation from DARPA, NASA, U.S. Navy, and DOE — provides credible third-party endorsement of technology
Fortress balance sheet: $38.6M cash, zero meaningful debt, 25+ quarters of runway even under stress
Capital-light fabless model with ~50-60% gross margins suggests attractive unit economics if commercial scale is achieved
Founder-CEO holds ~10% of shares (~$23.6M stake), providing meaningful incentive alignment
key_risks: 97% government contract revenue with $41K in commercial product revenue in Q2 FY2026 — six years post-founding with no commercial traction
Serial equity dilution: share count grew 45.9% in one year; $100M shelf registration (Form S-3) filed for future issuances
Single DARPA contract ($11.7M) dominates revenue; one missed technical milestone eliminates the primary revenue source
All insider transactions in the past 3 months are sales; no open-market purchases by any insider
Well-capitalized incumbents (Coherent, Lumentum, Hamamatsu, Intel Photonics) can redirect resources to III-V-on-silicon if market proves out
red_flags: Bear-case downside is -93% ($1/share) vs bull-case upside of +52% ($23/share) — deeply asymmetric to the downside
Stock trades at ~2x probability-weighted intrinsic value (~$7.44/share vs $15.02 market price)
Q2 FY2026 Adjusted EBITDA swung to -$917K from +$647K year-over-year — deteriorating near-term economics
CEO sold $902,500 in stock in same period an equity bonus grant vested — legal but poor optics
52-week price range of $5.79-$25.88 (347% spread) reflects venture-stage volatility, not investment-grade stability
buy_triggers: Stock price falls to $6-8/share, near the base-case probability-weighted intrinsic value, creating a genuine margin of safety
A named commercial customer (hyperscaler, defense prime, or major OEM) announces a development agreement or volume purchase order for photonic devices
DARPA contract milestones confirmed met on schedule AND one or more new government contracts totaling $5M+ awarded in a single quarter
sell_triggers: Commercial product revenue has not reached a $1M annualized run-rate by end of Q4 FY2026 (November 2026) — confirms continued commercialization slippage
Another dilutive equity raise that takes total shares above 20M without corresponding commercial revenue acceleration
CEO or major insider accelerates stock sales beyond disclosed 10b5-1 plan volumes, or any new insider selling program is filed at current price levels
valuation_summary: At $15.02/share and ~$283M market cap, ALMU trades at ~52x EV/FY2025 revenue of $4.7M with zero commercial product revenue. Probability-weighted intrinsic value is approximately $7.44/share (bear: $1.00 at 40%, base: $6.20 at 40%, bull: $22.80 at 20%), implying the stock is roughly 2x overvalued on an expected-value basis. Morningstar fair value is $22.20 but embeds optimistic base-to-bull assumptions. The market is pricing a >50% probability of the bull case when the evidence supports it at under 20%.
source_summary: Analysis based on web search results from March 2026 including Aeluma press releases (Q1-Q2 FY2026, FY2025 full year), SEC filings summaries (10-K, 10-Q via StockTitan), Morningstar, StockAnalysis, Yahoo Finance, BeyondSPX, MarketBeat, Nasdaq analyst commentary, Seeking Alpha, and investment thesis Substacks. No user-provided context files. Balance sheet data from December 31, 2025 (Q2 FY2026). Current price reference: ~$15.02 (early March 2026).
change_notes: Initial analysis. No prior report to compare against.
